Erasmus School of Social and Behavioural Sciences (ESSB) | Rotterdam, Netherlands
Research Assistant | Dec 2021 – Present
- Developed and managed datasets from international child surveys for an Erasmus+ project.
- Performed statistical analyses and data visualizations for publications and conferences.
- Created detailed R-Markdown documentation for reproducible workflows.
Erasmus MC Sophia Children’s Hospital | Rotterdam, Netherlands
Research Intern | Sep 2019 – Aug 2020
- Evaluated the quality of MRI data and conducted statistical analyses for research studies.
- Contributed to research manuscripts and reporting.
Leiden Academic Centre for Drug Research (LACDR) | Leiden, Netherlands
Research Intern | Mar 2016 – Jul 2016
- Investigated the pharmacokinetic profile of paracetamol in zebrafish larvae.
- Designed experiments, analyzed data, and presented findings.

title: Rotterdam Gold
text:
Project Overview
Evaluate longitudinal survey data to understand the impact of school interventions (“focus” vs. “control” schools) on student well-being and coping strategies across four waves.






Publication



Rotterdam Goud: Eindrapport Pilot Rotterdams Goud: Wijzen van leren en sociaal-emotioneel welbevinden. Dijkstra, K., & el Ghani, A., (2024).

title: Abdelkarim el Ghani
text:
Abdelkarim el Ghani is a Research Assistant at the Erasmus University Rotterdam. His expertise lies in:

Statistical data analysis
Neuroscience
Open science practices

He is highly skilled in R programming, data visualization, and managing large-scale research datasets.
Abdelkarim has extensive experience in both academic and clinical research environments. He has:

Contributed to international conferences
Authored scientific publications
Supported open science initiatives by sharing code and workflows on platforms like the Open Science Framework (OSF).

His research interests include:

Neurodevelopment
Brain imaging
Statistical modeling of behavioral data

Driven by a passion for data-driven problem-solving, Abdelkarim aims to advance reproducible and transparent scientific research.
Photo by Henry & Co.



 Back to top

title: Abdelkarim el Ghani
text:
Welcome to My Portfolio!
I’m a statistical data analyst specializing in uncovering meaningful insights from complex datasets. My work lies at the intersection of neuroscience and data science, with a focus on statistical modeling, data visualization, and managing large-scale research initiatives.
With expertise in analyzing neurodevelopmental data and driving reproducible research, I aim to support data-driven solutions that enhance our understanding of human behavior and scientific processes.
To dive into the projects I’ve worked on, visit my Projects page, where you’ll find detailed insights into the work I’ve done, ranging from statistical modeling to data visualization.
If you’d like to know more about my background, skills, and experience, check out the About Me page.
Let’s connect and explore how statistical analysis can contribute to impactful research and informed decision-making.




 Back to top

title: FAIR-play
text:
Project Overview
The FAIRPLAY project, in collaboration with Nederlands Jeugdinstituut (NJi) and several top European football clubs, investigates the societal impact of football in fostering resilience and life skills in youth. Supported by an Erasmus+ grant, this three-year initiative works with Feyenoord, Charlton Athletic, Werder Bremen, Inter Milan, and Hibernian to develop and implement pedagogical strategies for coaches and youth workers. The project aims to empower young people, particularly in urban environments, by using football as a tool to teach resilience and life skills, culminating in a training manual for best practices and evidence-based approaches.


Results







Research Publication



Resilience among youth in soccer. Dijkstra, K., & el Ghani, A., (2024).
